title: Micropera rostrata
intro: Micropera rostrata, also known as the "Tongkat Ali of the Himalayas," is a plant species belonging to the family Acanthaceae. It has been traditionally used to alleviate various health issues, including stress and fatigue, due to its adaptogenic and antioxidant properties, which can help to regulate the body's response to stress and promote overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Micropera rostrata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Micropera rostrata is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and supplements, which are popular among those seeking to improve their mental clarity, physical performance, and resilience to stress.
taxonomy: Micropera rostrata is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family is one of the largest and most diverse groups of flowering plants, consisting of over 30,000 species. Micropera rostrata is further classified within the subfamily Orchidoideae, which contains many epiphytic and terrestrial orchids. At the tribe level, it falls within the tribe Aeridinae, a group characterized by its unique flower morphology. The specific classification of Micropera rostrata is as follows: Micropera rostrata, subtribe Aeridinae, tribe Aeridinae, subfamily Orchidoideae, family Orchidaceae.
uses: Micropera rostrata has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant's extracts have been found to have antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. In traditional medicine, Micropera rostrata is used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
